summary:
The contractile parts of intrafusal fibers are found at their terminal ends, where they can respond to gamma motor neuron input. These end portions contain myofibrillar structures capable of shortening and generating tension during neuromuscular activity. 

The summary must be strictly factual without adding interpretation or opinion beyond what is directly stated in the provided text.
